_readme: Mimora user settings. Edit by hand; restart the app to apply. Delete a key (or this whole file) to fall back to the built-in default. Keys starting with '_' are ignored and may be used as comments.
_color_theme: UI color theme: 'dark' or 'light'. Loads config/themes/<name>_schema.json; restart the app to apply.
_practice_language: Practice language: 'english' or 'spanish'. Selects the language profile (variants, voices, engines, starter text). Restart to apply. Also changeable in the settings window ('Language').
_accent: Regional variant of the practice language: 'american' or 'british' for English, 'castilian' for Spanish. Drives the TTS voices and the espeak dialect used for pronunciation scoring; must belong to practice_language. Restart to apply. (Replaces the legacy 'english_accent' key, which is still read as a fallback.)
_voice: Default TTS voice; null means the accent's default (af_heart / bf_emma). Must belong to the selected accent. American: af_heart, af_bella, af_nicole, af_sarah, af_sky, am_adam, am_michael, am_echo, am_eric, am_liam. British: bf_emma, bf_alice, bf_isabella, bf_lily, bm_george, bm_daniel, bm_fable, bm_lewis.
_random_voice: Pick a fresh random voice of the current accent for every new phrase (never the one just heard). The pick is ephemeral: 'voice' above is kept unchanged and is used again when this is off. Note: a voice's data is downloaded on its first use, so the first pick of an unused voice delays that one generation. Also changeable in the settings window ('Random voice per phrase').
_engine: Pronunciation engine: 'phoneme' (default; espeak reference + phoneme ASR), 'acoustic' (Wav2Vec2 embeddings + DTW), or 'none' (scoring off: skips the ~1.2 GB recognizer entirely, every take is accepted - for slow machines; compare the takes by ear). Restart to apply.
_phoneme_good_mode: Phoneme-engine GOOD anchor: 'global' (the default: one calibrated anchor for all phrases; the 0-5 buckets were calibrated under this mode) or 'ceiling' (per-phrase anchor from the reference audio, so a flawless read maps to 100 per phrase; costs an extra recognizer pass and shifts scores off the bucket calibration). Only used when the phoneme engine is active.
_max_record_seconds: Hard limit on a single recording, in seconds (safety stop for a stuck key).
_silence_timeout: Seconds of continuous silence (after speech has begun) before the take auto-stops. Minimum 0.5.
_silence_threshold: RMS level (0..1) strictly above which a chunk counts as speech rather than silence. Minimum 0.001. Raise it only if a noisy room keeps the take from stopping.
_save_recordings: Diagnostic recording dumps: when true, every take writes the spoken reference (model.wav), the raw mic capture (raw.wav), the normalized signal (normalized.wav) and the phrase text (phrase.txt) to records/, each overwritten per take so only the latest is kept. Off by default: it puts your voice on disk on every attempt.
_llm_backend: 'local_server' starts llm_server automatically; 'lm-studio' uses an already-running LM Studio app; 'off' loads no LLM at all - phrases are the practice text's own sentences, taken verbatim and in order (aimed primarily at slow machines: no GGUF model needed, less RAM/VRAM, faster start; the phrase_length setting is ignored). Restart to apply.
_external_model_path: GGUF model file for the local_server backend (unused with 'off'). Relative paths resolve against the project root.
_external_n_ctx: Context window size (n_ctx) for the local GGUF model. Overrides the hardware-detected value; delete the key to fall back to it.
_practice_text_file: Source text shown in the input panel at startup. Relative paths resolve against the project root.
_practice_text_collapsed: Start with the practice-text box collapsed (the caption row stays visible and shows a short preview of the text). Also toggled by clicking the 'Practice text:' caption in the app. Collapsed by default.
_phrase_gen_level: Difficulty of generated phrases, 0-5: 0 = complete beginner (simplest words, present tense only), 5 = advanced (rich vocabulary, complex sentences). Applies from the next phrase; ignored when llm_backend is 'off'. Also changeable in the settings window ('Difficulty').
_phrase_gen_window_sentences: How many consecutive sentences of the source text are sent to the LLM per request (sliding window).
_phrase_gen_window_repeats: How many phrases are generated at one window position before the window slides forward by half its size.
_phrase_length: Phrase length to generate: 'full' (a complete sentence) or 'fragment' (a 2-4 word fragment). Ignored when llm_backend is 'off' (source-text sentences are never shortened). Also changeable in the settings window ('Phrase length').
_reference_speed: Reference playback speed, 0.7 to 1.4 in steps of 0.05 (1.0 = normal, lower is slower). Also changeable in the settings window ('Reference speed', a slider); the Slow ▶ button plays 0.1 below this value.
_playback_own_recording: Play the just-recorded take back to you before the score. On by default; when false the app skips straight to analysis (useful on slow machines). The manual 'listen to your recording' control is unaffected either way. Also changeable in the settings window ('Play back your recording').
_translation_language: Language the practice phrase is translated into and shown under the phrase in the hero card. '' means translation off. The choices depend on the practice language: '', English, Russian, Ukrainian, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Chinese, Japanese - minus the language being practiced (e.g. English practice drops English, Spanish practice drops Spanish). Loads the NLLB-200 model (~2.4 GB) when set. Also changeable in the settings window ('Translation').
_warm_up: Run a dummy pass over every loaded model at startup so the first take has no first-call latency. Off by default: startup is faster and the first take pays that latency instead; set to true to move the wait to startup.
_show_prosody: Show the intonation & stress block (pitch/F0 and energy charts together). Also toggled by the 'Intonation & stress' collapse header in the app and by the settings window. Collapsed by default: while collapsed the charts are hidden and the prosody computation is skipped, making analysis noticeably faster on slow machines.
_show_face: Show the articulation face in the hero card's score row. Toggled from the settings window ('Show articulation face').
_user_name: Display name of the practicing user; editable in the settings window ('Your name') and saved automatically.
